Kansas City · McAllen · San Diego · Oakland

Connection is
what matters.

Not long ago, I was the kid in the stands who would have done anything to get even a game-worn shin guard. That never left me. Everything I do in the community is about giving back what the game — and the people in it — gave to me.

Connection is everything

I was that kid once.
Not long ago.

I remember being young, watching professional players walk off the pitch, hoping — just hoping — that one of them would look up. That one of them would sign something, say something, acknowledge that you were there. To a kid, that moment is everything. It stays with you forever.

I am that professional now. And I have never forgotten what it felt like to be on the other side of that barrier. The connection between a player and the people who show up for them is rare, and it is sacred. It is not something I take lightly — not for a single second.

To have built genuine relationships with fans, with cities, with communities — that is not common. And I do not take it for granted. I am truly blessed to have had my dreams realized. This is how I give that back.

"I was that kid once — not long ago. The one who would have done anything for a game-worn shin guard. I have never forgotten what that felt like."
Camden Riley
Oakland Charter High School · Class of 2025 · Keynote Address
Keynote speaker

Oakland Charter High School.
Their first choice.

In 2025, Camden was invited by Oakland Charter High School to deliver the keynote commencement address to their graduating Class of 2025. The invitation came directly from the school — they had encountered him at prior community events and reached out to Oakland Roots requesting him specifically.

Standing in front of graduates, families, and teachers, Camden spoke about purpose, setback, and what it really means to show up — not just for yourself, but for the people around you.

"Living with purpose doesn't always look flashy. It's how you show up for your people. It's how you stay rooted in your truth. And it's how you rise — and bring others with you."

— Camden Riley · Oakland Charter HS Commencement, 2025
San Diego · After the final whistle
CR Academy · Youth coaching session
CR Academy · Training session
San Diego · Street soccer event
Oakland · School visit
Labor advocacy

The first-ever USL Championship
labor agreement.

In 2020, Camden became part of a small group of players who helped negotiate and ratify the first-ever Collective Bargaining Agreement in USL Championship history. There were no existing standards. No template. No precedent.

His role required communicating complex labor rights across a wildly diverse player population — from 16-year-old prospects who had never graduated high school, to veterans who didn't speak English, to senior players with families and mortgages. He had to hold the line in rooms with club owners and league executives with significant institutional leverage.

The CBA was ratified. For the first time in USL history, there were binding professional standards for players. That is not a small thing.

What this meant
"For the first time, every player in the USL Championship had real rights. That's what community looks like at scale."
Oakland Roots · Coliseum Return Press Conference
Four cities. One commitment.

Wherever the game took him,
he showed up for the city.

2015 – 2018
Stockton, CA
  • Head coach, UoP Honors Society soccer team
  • Top Soccer program — inclusive coaching for youth with special education needs
  • Community centered on belonging and inclusion
2019 – 2021
Kansas City & McAllen
  • Community appearances with Sporting Kansas City
  • Full immersion in McAllen's Spanish-speaking community
  • USL Championship CBA ratification — player advocacy at scale
  • MLSPA rookie symposium involvement
2022 – 2023
San Diego
  • Street soccer and futsal events across San Diego communities
  • Founded CR Academy — 250+ athletes across 4 camps
  • Live TV appearances on ABC 10News and Fox 5 San Diego
  • Partnerships with Darby Magazine and local brands
2024 – 2025
Oakland
  • Mr. Espresso Day of Giving — covered all customer purchases
  • Town Smacks Campaign — spotlighted local Oakland businesses
  • Veterans Kitchen — Day of Giving for military families
  • Keynote commencement address, Oakland Charter HS
  • Oakland Zoo & Alameda Pickleball community appearances
  • Roots Righteous Award — Man of the Year, 2025
The connections that define it all

I would be nowhere
without the people in my corner.

Every city. Every locker room. Every kid who stayed late after the final whistle. Every family who trusted me with their child. Every fan who wore the jersey. These are the relationships that define every chapter — not the trophies, not the appearances, not the accolades. The people.

🤝

The Players

Teammates who pushed me. Veterans who mentored me. Young players who trusted me to advocate for their rights in negotiations they didn't yet understand. Every locker room made me better.

⚽

The Kids

250+ athletes through CR Academy. School visits. Post-game autographs. Community clinics. I was that kid once — watching professionals and dreaming. I don't take that responsibility lightly.

🏙️

The Cities

Kansas City. McAllen. San Diego. Oakland. Four cities that shaped me as much as I tried to give back to them. I carry all of them with me. Oakland most of all — that city will always be home.

Let's build
something real.

The connection between a player and their community is rare. I've been lucky enough to experience it — and I want to keep building it. Available for speaking engagements, school visits, community partnerships, and youth mentorship. If it matters to your people, it matters to me.

Start a conversation →
